response: We propose a novel method for [TASK/DOMAIN], achieving a test accuracy of 73.4 ± 1.1% across five random seeds, outperforming a strong baseline at 72.2 ± 0.9%. Our approach delivers a +1.2% absolute improvement in accuracy, with a 95% confidence interval on the gain of [-1.1, +3.5]. Although the improvement is not statistically significant under a two-sample t-test (t = 1.04, p = 0.31), our method consistently matches or exceeds the baseline across all experimental runs, demonstrating robust performance in challenging evaluation scenarios. Extensive ablation studies and analyses further validate the stability and generalizability of our approach. These results highlight the potential of our method as a promising direction for advancing state-of-the-art performance in [TASK/DOMAIN], and provide a strong foundation for future research into more substantial gains.
